title: Umami Chewy Spanish Lamb Leg Stir-fry
description: This lazy Sunday dish features a chewy Umami flavor from miso paste and a tandoor-style searing to add depth of flavor to the vegan-friendly lamb leg.

ingredients.name: Lamb Leg
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's properly rested and sliced into bite-sized pieces.

ingredients.name: Miso Paste
ingredients.notes: Use a mild variety for less intense Umami flavor or try out some Korean or Japanese varieties.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Extra virgin if you have it, regular otherwise for a vegan substitute.

instructions: Place sliced lamb leg in a baking dish and sprinkle miso paste evenly over it.
Mash garlic cloves with a pinch of salt, then spread this mix on top of the miso-covered lamb.
Drizzle olive oil over the lamb and toss to coat evenly. Season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper if desired.
Bake for about 30 minutes or until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature should reach 145°F/63°C).
While the lamb is baking, in a separate pan on medium heat, add olive oil, minced shallots, and minced garlic. Stir-fry until translucent.
Add chopped sesame seeds to the stir-fried mixture for added crunch.
When the lamb is done, remove from oven and let it rest for about 10 minutes before slicing into pieces.
Serve hot with a drizzle of lime juice over the sliced lamb leg.
